name: Gulf AOR Border Crossings
description: Land border crossing status as of 2026-03-03

properties.name: Mezyad/Buraimi
properties.notes:
Open and in use. Reports mention people crossing UAE–Oman borders, including in the Al Ain/Buraimi area, with new/additional corridors like Al Rawdah (related to Buraimi) helping relieve congestion. No closure reported. Last Note: 4March26.

Oman: new paperwork process is in place on the Oman side, increasing delays. Recommend planning 12+ hours at UAE–Oman crossings due to congestion, increased security, and processing timelines.

properties.name: Al Wajajah (Hatta)
properties.notes:
Open, though with very slow traffic and long delays (e.g., 2–6+ hours reported in some cases). Guides/updates from late Feb/early Mar 2026 confirm it’s operational for road travel to Oman, despite regional issues. Last Note: 4March26.

Oman: new paperwork process is in place on the Oman side, increasing delays. Recommend planning 12+ hours at UAE–Oman crossings due to congestion, increased security, and processing timelines.

properties.name: Khatmat Malaha
properties.notes:
No specific closure reports. General UAE–Oman land borders remain functional for rerouting (buses/private cars), though processing is slow. Last Note: 4March26.

Oman: new paperwork process is in place on the Oman side, increasing delays. Recommend planning 12+ hours at UAE–Oman crossings due to congestion, increased security, and processing timelines.

properties.name: Al Ghuwaifat/Silaa
properties.notes: Open and actively used. Etihad Rail ran exceptional passenger train services from Al Ghuwaifat (Saudi side) to Abu Dhabi to help stranded passengers return amid airspace issues—no indications of closure.

properties.name: Al Hadithah (Mudawwarah)
properties.notes: Nothing reported; assumed open. Last Note: 4March26.
properties.traffic_note: No route returned by Directions API

properties.name: Al Salmi / Nuwaiseeb
properties.notes: Nothing reported; assumed open. No closure noted.

properties.name: Al Wadiah
properties.notes: Open and operating normally. Recent reports (incl. aid trucks crossing in Jan 2026 and confirmations of regular operations) indicate it remains the main active Saudi–Yemen land link. Last Note: 4March26.
properties.traffic_note: No route returned by Directions API

properties.name: Sharurah
properties.notes: No closure reported; limited activity; assumed open. No closure; near Al Wadiah and part of the same region—operations appear normal. Last Note: 4March26.

properties.name: Arar
properties.notes: No current closure from recent events; past temporary shutdowns (e.g., 2025 for smuggling) but reopened for trade. Last Note: 4March26.

properties.name: King Fahd Causeway
properties.notes: Open / operational. Authorities confirmed smooth traffic and full capacity in both directions as of 4March26. Earlier unconfirmed reports of temporary closure/incidents (e.g., alleged drone activity) were not verified/were debunked. Some advisories warn against use due to threats, but current status is open and normal.

properties.name: Al Khajif
properties.notes: No specific info on closure. Last Note: 4March26.

properties.name: Umm Al Zamool
properties.notes: No closure reported; aligns with open Saudi–Oman borders.

properties.name: Al Wagan
properties.notes:
No specific mentions of closure; falls under the general pattern of UAE–Oman borders staying open but congested. Last Note: 4March26.

Oman: new paperwork process is in place on the Oman side, increasing delays. Recommend planning 12+ hours at UAE–Oman crossings due to congestion, increased security, and processing timelines.

properties.name: Khatm Al Skikla
properties.notes:
No closure reported; similar to other Oman crossings, likely open with delays. Last Note: 4March26.

Oman: new paperwork process is in place on the Oman side, increasing delays. Recommend planning 12+ hours at UAE–Oman crossings due to congestion, increased security, and processing timelines.

properties.name: Hamasa
properties.notes:
No specific closure info; part of the operational UAE–Oman network.

Oman: new paperwork process is in place on the Oman side, increasing delays. Recommend planning 12+ hours at UAE–Oman crossings due to congestion, increased security, and processing timelines.

properties.name: Al Ain Border Post
properties.notes:
Open. Refers to the general Al Ain area crossings (e.g., Mezyad/Buraimi)—open. Last Note: 4March26.

Oman: new paperwork process is in place on the Oman side, increasing delays. Recommend planning 12+ hours at UAE–Oman crossings due to congestion, increased security, and processing timelines.

properties.notes:
Limited info, but no reports of closure; diplomatic channels may have specific access.

Oman: new paperwork process is in place on the Oman side, increasing delays. Recommend planning 12+ hours at UAE–Oman crossings due to congestion, increased security, and processing timelines.
